items.title: Iran demands a ceasefire in Lebanon before peace talks
items.summary: Iran's ‌parliament speaker, Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf, said in a post on X on Friday that two previously agreed measures, a ceasefire in Lebanon and the release of Iran's blocked assets, must be implemented before negotiations begin.

items.title: Oil: Fragile ceasefire keeps supply risks elevated – Wells Fargo
items.summary: Wells Fargo’s international economics team notes that a fragile ceasefire in the Middle East leaves Oil market risks elevated and conviction on the outlook low.

items.title: MXN: Dovish Banxico and depreciation outlook – Commerzbank
items.summary: Commerzbank’s Michael Pfister and Norman Liebke note that Mexico’s latest inflation data and Banxico minutes support a dovish stance.

items.title: India: Outlook stays subdued on external risks – Standard Chartered
items.summary: Standard Chartered Bank economists Saurav Anand and Anubhuti Sahay highlight a sharp slowdown in India’s new investment announcements in March, heavily affected by the Middle East conflict and sector-specific issues in renewable electricity and chemicals.

items.title: DXY: Shipping risk, inflation path and Fed easing – BBH
items.summary: Brown Brothers Harriman’s (BBH) Elias Haddad notes that global markets are cautious ahead of US-Iran ceasefire talks, with Brent, equities and bonds reacting while the Dollar stabilizes.

items.title: Oil: Physical tightness and supply shocks reshape pricing – BNY
items.summary: BNY’s Head of Markets Macro Strategy Bob Savage highlights extreme dislocation in Oil markets, with North Sea Forties Blend near $147/bbl and Dated Brent far above futures as Iran’s control of the Strait of Hormuz cuts flows to 8% of normal.

items.title: US: War burden and spending power – UBS
items.summary: UBS economist Paul Donovan discusses how US March consumer price inflation highlights US consumers’ war-related burden and affordability concerns.

items.title: BRL: Hawkish tilt and supported real – Commerzbank
items.summary: Commerzbank analysts Michael Pfister and Norman Liebke highlight that Brazil’s inflation edged higher in February, and they expect today’s March data to show another fairly sharp rise in prices.

items.title: WTI Crude Oil nears $93.00, with the Strait of Hormuz practically closed
items.summary: Oil prices are ticking up for the second consecutive day on Friday. The US benchmark West Texas Intermediate (WTI) Oil has returned to levels near $93.00 per barrel, as restrictions in the Strait of Hormuz keep a de facto blockade in place, adding strain to an already frail ceasefire deal in Iran.

items.title: USD: Inflation and peace risks shape Dollar – ING
items.summary: ING’s Francesco Pesole notes that the US Dollar Index remains just below 99.0, with further downside seen if a permanent Middle East peace deal is agreed and Strait of Hormuz flows resume.

items.title: IDF remains in state of war with Lebanon
items.summary: According to a report from the Guardian, Israel Defense Forces (IDF) chief of staff, Eyal Zamir, said that forces are continuing their combat operations in southern Lebanon and are “not in a ceasefire” with Hezbollah.

items.title: US equities: Outlook-driven earnings season – BNY
items.summary: BNY’s Head of Markets Macro Strategy Bob Savage argues that Q1 US equities will be driven more by forward guidance than reported earnings, with consensus S&P 500 earnings growth around 13% and wide dispersion in forecasts.
